title: senecio leucophyllus subsp. incanus
intro: Senecio leucophyllus subsp. incanus, also known as the woolly Jacob's ladder or woolly ragwort, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Senecio leucophyllus subsp. incanus include its potential to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and aid in weight loss. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
